expectations.text: Correctly classifies the energy company as an Essential Entity (EE) under NIS2 Annex I (energy sector, electricity sub-sector)
expectations.evidence: Without-skill response incorrectly classified the DSO as 'Important Entity (most likely)' despite DSOs being explicitly named in NIS2 Annex I (Essential Entities). The response suggested authorities 'could' designate it as EE based on criticality, which misunderstands the Annex I automatic classification.

expectations.text: Confirms the entity meets size thresholds (≥50 employees OR ≥€10M turnover) making it automatically in scope
expectations.evidence: Response correctly identifies 200 employees and €50M turnover as exceeding the NIS2 size thresholds for medium+ entities.

expectations.text: Explains Art. 20 governance obligation: management body approval, training, and potential personal liability
expectations.evidence: Response covers Art. 20 management accountability and personal liability obligations.

expectations.text: Lists all 10 Art. 21 cybersecurity risk management measures or explicitly covers the key ones (incident handling, BCP, supply chain, MFA, cryptography)
expectations.evidence: Response covers the 10 Art. 21 measures including supply chain, MFA, cryptography, incident handling, and BCP/DR.

expectations.text: States Art. 23 incident reporting timelines: 24-hour early warning, 72-hour notification, 1-month final report to CSIRT/competent authority
expectations.evidence: Response correctly states the 24h early warning, 72h full notification, and 1-month final report timeline to CSIRT/competent authority.
